name: microsoft-defender-threat-intelligence
label: Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence
description: The Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence FortiSOAR Connector enables automated threat intelligence gathering, enrichment, and response within FortiSOAR

fields.title: Get Access Token
fields.name: auth_type
fields.tooltip: Select the authentication method for accessing security graph APIs.
fields.description: Select the method to obtain authentication tokens for accessing the security graph APIs. Options: Delegate Permission, Application Permission, or Certificate-Based Authentication.
options: On behalf of User - Delegate Permission

Without a User - Application Permission.title: Server URL
Without a User - Application Permission.name: resource
Without a User - Application Permission.description: Specify the service-based URI to connect and perform automated operations.
Without a User - Application Permission.tooltip: Enter the service URL for API communication.

Without a User - Application Permission.title: Tenant ID
Without a User - Application Permission.description: Specify the ID of the tenant assigned to you by the Azure application registration portal.
Without a User - Application Permission.name: tenant

Without a User - Application Permission.title: Client ID
Without a User - Application Permission.description: Specify the Unique Application ID from Azure Active Directory to generate authentication tokens.
Without a User - Application Permission.name: client_id
Without a User - Application Permission.tooltip: Enter the Azure Application ID for authentication.

Without a User - Application Permission.title: Client Secret
Without a User - Application Permission.description: Specify the Unique Client Secret of the Azure AD application for authentication. For information on how to get the secret key, see https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/threat-protection/microsoft-defender-atp/exposed-apis-create-app-webapp.
Without a User - Application Permission.name: client_secret
Without a User - Application Permission.tooltip: Enter the Client Secret for authentication.

On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.title: Server URL
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.name: resource
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.description: Specify the service-based URI to connect and perform automated operations.
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.tooltip: Specify the service-based URI to connect and perform automated operations.

On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.title: Tenant ID
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.description: Specify the ID of the tenant assigned to you by the Azure application registration portal.
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.tooltip: Specify the ID of the tenant assigned to you by the Azure application registration portal.
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.name: tenant

On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.title: Client ID
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.description: Specify the Unique Application ID of the Azure Active Directory application to create an authentication token required to access the API. For information on getting authentication tokens, see the Getting Authentication Tokens section.
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.name: client_id
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.tooltip: Specify the Unique Application ID of the Azure Active Directory application to create an authentication token required to access the API. For information on getting authentication tokens, see the Getting Authentication Tokens section.

On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.title: Client Secret
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.description: Specify the Unique Client Secret of the Azure Active Directory application that is used to create an authentication token required to access the API. For information on how to get the secret key, see https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/threat-protection/microsoft-defender-atp/exposed-apis-create-app-webapp.
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.name: client_secret
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.tooltip: Specify the Unique Client Secret of the Azure Active Directory application that is used to create an authentication token required to access the API.

On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.title: Authorization Code
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.name: code
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.description: Enter the authorization code obtained during authentication.

On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.title: Redirect URL
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.name: redirect_url
On behalf of User - Delegate Permission.description: Specify the registered redirect URI for authentication responses.

fields.title: Verify SSL
fields.description: Indicates whether the SSL certificate for the server should be verified. Default: True.
fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.tooltip: Enable or disable SSL certificate verification.

operations.title: Get Host Reputation
operations.description: Retrieves security reputation and threat intelligence data for a specified host to assess its risk level and potential malicious activity.

parameters.title: Host ID
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the host, which can be an IP address or Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN), used to retrieve its security reputation and threat intelligence data.
parameters.name: hostId
parameters.tooltip: Enter an IP or FQDN to retrieve reputation data.

operations.title: Get Host Details
operations.description: Retrieves security reputation and threat intelligence details for a specified host using its IP address or Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN).

parameters.title: Host ID
parameters.description: Unique identifier for the host, which can be an IP address or Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN), used to retrieve its security reputation and threat intelligence data.
parameters.name: hostId
parameters.tooltip: Enter an IP or FQDN to retrieve host details.

operations.title: Get Whois Record
operations.description: Retrieves domain registration and ownership information through Whois lookup, either by host identifier or specific Whois record ID.

parameters.title: Select Host ID or whoisRecord ID
parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Choose whether to retrieve Whois information using a host identifier or a specific Whois record ID.
parameters.tooltip: Select Host ID or Whois Record ID.

Host ID.title: Host ID
Host ID.description: Enter an IP or FQDN to retrieve Whois registration information.
Host ID.name: hostId
Host ID.tooltip: Enter an IP or FQDN for Whois lookup.

whoisRecord ID.title: whoisRecord ID
whoisRecord ID.name: whoisRecordId
whoisRecord ID.description: Enter a specific Whois record identifier to retrieve detailed registration information.

operations.title: Get Components List
operations.description: Retrieves an inventory of software components, services, and applications detected on the specified host system.

parameters.title: Host ID
parameters.description: Enter an IP or FQDN to list detected software components and services.
parameters.name: hostId
parameters.tooltip: Enter an IP or FQDN to view host components.

operations.title: Get Passive DNS List
operations.description: Retrieves historical DNS resolution records showing how domain names have mapped to IP addresses over time.

parameters.title: Host ID
parameters.description: Enter an IP or FQDN to retrieve historical DNS resolution records.
parameters.name: hostId
parameters.tooltip: Enter an IP or FQDN for DNS history.

operations.title: Get Passive DNS Reverse List
operations.description: Retrieves historical reverse DNS records showing how IP addresses have mapped to domain names over time.

parameters.title: Host ID
parameters.description: Enter an IP or FQDN to retrieve reverse DNS resolution records.
parameters.name: hostId
parameters.tooltip: Enter an IP or FQDN for reverse DNS lookup.

operations.title: Get Host Ports List
operations.description: Retrieves a comprehensive list of open ports, associated services, and network protocols active on the specified host.

parameters.title: Host ID
parameters.description: Enter an IP or FQDN to list open ports and services running on the host.
parameters.name: hostId
parameters.tooltip: Enter an IP or FQDN to list open ports.

operations.title: Get Host SSL Certificates List
operations.description: Retrieves all SSL/TLS certificates associated with the host, including validity dates, issuers, and certificate details.

parameters.title: Host ID
parameters.description: Enter an IP or FQDN to retrieve SSL/TLS certificates associated with the host.
parameters.name: hostId
parameters.tooltip: Enter an IP or FQDN to view SSL certificates.

operations.title: Get Host SSL Certificate
operations.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific SSL/TLS certificate, including chain of trust, cryptographic details, and validation status.

parameters.title: Host Certificate ID
parameters.description: Enter the unique SSL certificate identifier to retrieve details such as chain of trust and validation status.
parameters.name: hostSslCertificateId
parameters.tooltip: Enter the certificate ID for SSL details.

operations.title: Get Host Component
operations.description: Retrieves detailed information about a specific software component, including version, configuration, and security status.

parameters.title: Host Component ID
parameters.description: Enter the component identifier to retrieve software version, configuration, and security details.
parameters.name: hostComponentId
parameters.tooltip: Enter the component ID for details.

operations.title: Get Indicators List
operations.description: Retrieves a comprehensive list of threat indicators, including IOCs, behavioral patterns, and security alerts from the specified intelligence profile.

parameters.title: Intelligence Profile ID
parameters.description: Enter the intelligence profile identifier to retrieve associated threat indicators.
parameters.name: intelligenceProfileId

playbook_collections.name: Sample - Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence - 1.0.0
playbook_collections.description: Sample playbooks for "Microsoft Defender Threat Intelligence" connector. If you are planning to use any of the sample playbooks in your environment, ensure that you clone those playbooks and move them to a different collection, since the sample playbook collection gets deleted during connector upgrade and delete.
